blocks:
Sūrat al-Nabaʾ

Meccan, and it is 40 verses.

Sūrat 'Amma

It is called Sūrat al-Nabaʾ And it is Meccan; the number of its verses is forty-one verses according to the Meccan and Basran schools. And forty according to the rest. Their difference lies in عَذاباً قَرِيباً (a near punishment) [counted as one verse] according to the Meccan and Basran schools. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)** عَمَ ** (1) About what Its origin is عن ما يَتَساءَلُونَ (about what they question). Some of them question others. In this interrogation is an exaltation of the matter about which they question the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(٢) عَنِ النَّبَإِ الْعَظِيمِ. (2) concerning the great news,

(٣) الَّذِي هُمْ فِيهِ مُخْتَلِفُونَ ** (3) about which they differ? This is an explanation of the exalted matter. It is said that they were questioning about the Resurrection (البعث).

In al-Kāfī, narrated from al-Sādiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse, He said: النَّبَإِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (the great news) is the Wilāyah (Guardianship).

And from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about the interpretation of عَمَّ يَتَساءَلُونَ (About what do they question?), and He said: "It is concerning the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) used to say: "There is no sign (آية) of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, greater than me, nor is there any news (نبأ) of Allah greater than me."

And al-Qummiyy narrated from al-Ridā (peace be upon him): That He was asked about it, and He said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: "There is no news (نبأ) of Allah greater than me, nor is there any sign (آية) of Allah greater than me. My merit (فضلي) was indeed presented to the past nations, despite the difference in their languages, but they did not acknowledge my merit."

And in al-Uyun, narrated from Him, from His father, from His forefathers, from al-Husayn ibn ‘Alī (peace be upon them), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said to ‘Alī (peace be upon him): "O ‘Alī, you are the Proof (حجّة) of Allah, and you are the Gate (باب) of Allah, and you are the Path (الطريق) to Allah, and you are the النَّبَإِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (the great news), and you are the Straight Path (الصراط المستقيم), and you are the Highest Example (المثل الأعلى)." (The rest of) the narrative (الحديث).

And in al-Kāfī, in the Sermon of al-Wasīlah (the Means) by the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "I am the **النَّبَإِ الْعَظِيمِ ** (the great news), and soon you will know what you are promised."

(٤) كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ ** (4) No indeed! Soon they will know! A deterrent against the questioning and a threat concerning it. (٥) ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَيَعْلَمُونَ ** (5) Again, no indeed! Soon they will know! A repetition for emphasis, and the word ثُمَّ (then) is to indicate that the second threat is more severe. It was also recited with the ت (تَعْلَمُونَ). (٦) أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ الْأَرْضَ مِهاداً ** (6) Did We not make the earth a resting place, For mankind. (٧) وَالْجِبالَ أَوْتاداً ** (7) and the mountains as stakes, For the earth. (٨) وَخَلَقْناكُمْ أَزْواجاً ** (8) and created you in pairs, Male and female. (٩) وَجَعَلْنا نَوْمَكُمْ سُباتاً ** (9) and made your sleep a rest, A cessation of sensation and movement, for the rest of the faculties. (١٠) وَجَعَلْنَا اللَّيْلَ لِباساً ** (10) and made the night a covering, A covering that conceals, with its darkness, whoever wishes to hide. Al-Qummi said: It covers the day. (١١) وَجَعَلْنَا النَّهارَ مَعاشاً ** (11) and made the day for livelihood? A time for livelihood, wherein you move about to acquire that by which you live. (١٢) وَبَنَيْنا فَوْقَكُمْ سَبْعاً شِداداً ** (12) And We built above you seven firm heavens, Seven strong, well-constructed heavens, which the passage of ages does not affect. (١٣) وَجَعَلْنا سِراجاً وَهَّاجاً ** (13) and made a blazing lamp, Glittering and intensely burning, meaning the sun. (١٤) وَأَنْزَلْنا مِنَ الْمُعْصِراتِ (14) and sent down from the rain-laden clouds It is said: The clouds when they are squeezed (عُصِرَت), meaning they are about to be squeezed by the winds, so they rain. Al-Qummi said: From the clouds, ماءً ثَجَّاجاً ** (abundant water), pouring forth profusely. (١٥) لِنُخْرِجَ بِهِ حَبًّا وَنَباتاً ** (15) abundant water, that We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation, That which is consumed as food and that which is used as fodder, such as straw and grass. (١٦) وَجَنَّاتٍ أَلْفافاً ** (16) and luxuriant gardens? Intertwined, one part with another. (١٧) إِنَّ يَوْمَ الْفَصْلِ كانَ مِيقاتاً ** (17) Indeed the Day of Separation is an appointed time, A limit (حَدّاً) by which the world is timed and at which it ends, or a limit for the creatures to which they proceed. (١٨) يَوْمَ يُنْفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ فَتَأْتُونَ أَفْواجاً ** (18) the day the Trumpet is blown, and you come in groups, Groups from the graves to the gathering place (المَحْشَر).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: That He was asked about this verse, and He said: "Ten categories of my Ummah will be gathered scattered, whom Allah has distinguished from the Muslims and whose forms He has changed. Some of them will be in the form of apes (القِرَدَة), and some in the form of swine (الخَنَازِير). Some will be inverted, their legs above and their faces below, and then they will be dragged upon them. Some will be blind, stumbling about. Some will be deaf and dumb, unable to reason. Some will chew their tongues, with pus flowing from their mouths as saliva, which the people of the gathering (أَهْلُ الجَمْع) will find repulsive. Some will have their hands and feet cut off. Some will be crucified on trunks of fire. Some will have a stench worse than corpses (الجِيَف). And some will wear long garments (جِبَاباً سَابِغَة) of tar (قَطْرَان) clinging to their skins. As for those in the form of apes (القِرَدَة), they are the slanderers (القَتَّات) among mankind. As for those in the form of swine (الخَنَازِير), they are the people of unlawful gain (السُّحْت). As for those inverted upon their heads, they are the devourers of usury (آكِلَة الرِّبَا). The blind are those who are unjust in judgment (الجَائِرُونَ فِي الحُكْم). The deaf and dumb are those who are conceited with their deeds (المُعْجَبُونَ بِأَعْمَالِهِمْ). Those who chew their tongues are the scholars (العُلَمَاء) and judges (القُضَاة) whose deeds contradict their sayings. Those whose hands and feet are cut off are those who harm their neighbors (الجِيرَان). Those crucified on trunks of fire are those who inform the ruler (السُّلْطَان) about people (slanderers). Those with a stench worse than corpses (الجِيَف) are those who indulge in desires and pleasures and withhold the right of Allah, the Exalted, in their wealth. And those who wear the garments (الجِبَاب) are the people of pride (الفَخْر) and arrogance (الخُيَلَاء)."

(١٩) وَفُتِحَتِ السَّماءُ فَكانَتْ أَبْواباً ** (19) and the heaven is opened, and becomes gates, قيل شقّتْ شقوقاً والقمّيّ قال انفتح أبواب الجنان. It was said that it is split with fissures (شقوقاً). And al-Qummi said: The gates of the Gardens (الجنان) are opened.

(٢٠) وَسُيِّرَتِ الْجِبالُ فَكانَتْ سَراباً ** (20) and the mountains are set moving, and become a mirage. قال تسيّر الجبال مثل السراب الذي يلمع في المفازة. He said: The mountains are set moving like the mirage (السراب) which shimmers in the desert (المفازة).

(٢١) إِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ كانَتْ مِرْصاداً ** (21) Indeed, hell has been a lurking place, موضع رصد القمّيّ قائمة. A place of observation (موضع رصد). Al-Qummi said: Standing (قائمة).

(٢٢) لِلطَّاغِينَ مَآباً ** (22) a resort for the rebellious, مرجعاً ومأوى. A return (مرجعاً) and a refuge (مأوى).

(٢٣) لابِثِينَ فِيها ** (23) where they will remain for ages. وقرئ لبثين أَحْقاباً دهوراً متتابعة القمّيّ قال الأحقاب السنون والحقب السنة والسنة عددها ثلاثمائة وستّون يوماً واليوم كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. And it was read (لبثين). أَحْقاباً ** (ages): successive epochs (دهوراً متتابعة). Al-Qummi said: The ahqab (الأحقاب) are years (السنون), and the huqb (الحقب) is a year (السنة), and the number of days in a year is three hundred and sixty days, and the day is كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. (like a thousand years of what you count.)

وفي المعاني عن الصادق عليه السلام قال : الأحقاب ثمانية حقب والحقب ثمانون سنة والسنة ثلاث مائة وستّون يوماً واليوم كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. In al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The ahqab (الأحقاب) are eight huqb (حقب), and the huqb (الحقب) is eighty years, and the year is three hundred and sixty days, and the day is كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ. (like a thousand years of what you count.)

في المجمع عن النبيّ صلّى الله عليه وآله : لا يخرج من النّار من دخلها حتى يمكث فيها احقاباً والحقب بضع وستّون سنة والسنة ثلاثمائة وستّون يوماً كلّ يوم كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ فلا يتكلنّ أحد على أن يخرج من النّار. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): No one who enters the Fire shall leave it until he remains therein for ahqab (احقاباً). And the huqb (الحقب) is sixty-odd years, and the year is three hundred and sixty days, every day being كَأَلْفِ سَنَةٍ مِمَّا تَعُدُّونَ (like a thousand years of what you count). So let no one rely upon leaving the Fire.

وعن العيّاشي عن الباقر عليه السلام : أنّه سئل عن هذه الآية فقال هذه في الذين يخرجون من النّار. And from al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and he said: This is concerning those who will leave the Fire.

(٢٤) لا يَذُوقُونَ فِيها بَرْداً وَلا شَراباً. (24) They will not taste therein any coolness nor any drink, (٢٥) إِلَّا حَمِيماً وَغَسَّاقاً ** (25) except boiling water and pus, It was said that what is meant by al-bard (البرد) is that which refreshes them and relieves them from the heat of the Fire. And al-Qummi narrated: Bard (برداً) means sleep (نوماً). He said: Al-bard (البرد) is sleep (النّوم). And the explanation of al-ghassāq (الغسّاق) has already passed in Sūrat Ṣād. And it was recited with tashdīd (بالتشديد). (٢٦) جَزاءً وِفاقاً ** (26) a commensurate requital. Corresponding to their deeds and their beliefs. (٢٧) إِنَّهُمْ كانُوا لا يَرْجُونَ حِساباً. (27) Indeed, they did not expect any reckoning, (٢٨) وَكَذَّبُوا بِآياتِنا كِذَّاباً ** (28) and they vehemently denied Our signs. A denial (تكذيباً). And in al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Kidhābā (كذاباً) with takhfīf (بالتخفيف) means lying (الكذب). It was said that it was placed in the position of denial (التكذيب) to indicate that they lied in their denial. (٢٩) وَكُلَّ شَيْءٍ أَحْصَيْناهُ (١) كِتاباً ** (29) And We have enumerated everything in writing. An interjection (اعتراض). (٣٠) فَذُوقُوا فَلَنْ نَزِيدَكُمْ إِلَّا عَذاباً ** (30) So taste! We will not increase you except in punishment. Due to your disbelief in the punishment and your denial of the signs. And its occurrence via the method of iltifāt (الالتفات) is for emphasis. This verse is reported to be the severest in the Quran against the people of the Fire. (٣١) إِنَّ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ مَفازاً ** (31) Indeed, for the Godwary there is a triumph: Al-Qummi said: They will triumph (يفوزون). And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the honors (الكرامات). (٣٢) حَدائِقَ وَأَعْناباً ** (32) gardens and vineyards, Gardens containing various kinds of fruit-bearing trees. (٣٣) وَكَواعِبَ ** (33) and full-breasted maidens, Women whose breasts have become round (فلكت ثديهنّ), atrābā (أَتْراباً) meaning peers (لدات) of the same age (على سنّ واحد). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding: وَكَواعِبَ أَتْراباً ** (and full-breasted maidens, peers), meaning the young women whose breasts are swelling (الفتيات النّاهدات). (٣٤) وَكَأْساً دِهاقاً ** (34) and overflowing cups. Full (ممتلئة). (٣٥) لا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيها لَغْواً وَلا كِذَّاباً ** (35) They will not hear therein any vain talk or denial, And it was recited with takhfīf (بالتّخفيف), meaning lying (كذباً) or mutual lying (مكاذبة), meaning they will not lie to one another. (٣٦) جَزاءً مِنْ رَبِّكَ ** (36) a reward from your Lord, In accordance with His promise, ‘aṭā’an ḥisābā (عَطاءً حِساباً), meaning sufficient (كافياً). In al-Amālī, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) in a ḥadīth, he said: "Until when the Day of Resurrection comes, their good deeds will be reckoned for them, then He will give them for every single one ten times its like, up to seven hundredfold." Allah the Almighty said: جَزاءً مِنْ رَبِّكَ عَطاءً حِساباً ** (a reward from your Lord, a sufficient gift), and He said: فَأُولئِكَ لَهُمْ جَزاءُ الضِّعْفِ بِما عَمِلُوا.** (Those—there is a double reward for them for what they did.)

(١) Meaning, and every single thing of the deeds We have made clear in the Preserved Tablet (اللوح المحفوظ).

(٣٧) رَبِّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُمَا الرَّحْمنِ ** (37) the Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, the All-beneficent. And it was recited with the nominative case (بالرفع) in both (words). (They do not possess) except reward or punishment, because they are absolutely owned by Him, so they do not deserve to object to Him. And that does not negate intercession with His permission.

(٣٨) يَوْمَ يَقُومُ الرُّوحُ وَالْمَلائِكَةُ صَفًّا لا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمنُ وَقالَ صَواباً ** (38) The day the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks, they will not speak, except him whom the All-beneficent permits, and who speaks rightly. Al-Qummi said: الرُّوحُ (the Spirit) is an angel greater than Gabriel (جبرئيل) and Michael (ميكائيل), peace be upon them both. He was with the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and He is with the Imams, peace be upon them. And he narrated it in al-Majma' from al-Qummi, from al-Sadiq, peace be upon him. And in it (the Tafsir of al-Qummi), it is narrated from him (al-Sadiq), peace be upon him. And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim, peace be upon him: "By Allah, we are those who are permitted (to speak) on the Day of Resurrection, and those who speak rightly (صَواباً)." It was asked: "What will you say when you speak?" They said: "We will glorify our Lord, and send blessings upon our Prophet, and intercede for our followers (شيعتنا), and our Lord will not reject us."

(٣٩) ذلِكَ الْيَوْمُ الْحَقُ ** (39) That is the Day of the Truth. which will inevitably come to pass. فَمَنْ شاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ مَآباً ** So whoever wishes may take the path to his Lord. through faith (الإِيمان) and obedience (الطاعة). (٤٠) إِنَّا أَنْذَرْناكُمْ عَذاباً قَرِيباً ** (40) Indeed, We have warned you of a near punishment, meaning the punishment of the Hereafter (عذاب الآخرة). Its nearness is due to its certainty, for indeed, what is coming is near. Or, its beginning is death. al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said it refers to the Fire (النّار). (٤١) يَوْمَ يَنْظُرُ الْمَرْءُ ما قَدَّمَتْ يَداهُ ** (41) the day man will observe what his hands have sent ahead, of good or evil. وَيَقُولُ الْكافِرُ يا لَيْتَنِي كُنْتُ تُراباً ** and the faithless one will say, 'I wish I were dust!' (He wishes this) in the world, so that he had not been created and not been obligated (with duties), or (he wishes this) on that Day, so that he had not been resurrected. In al-Ilal (العلل), narrated from Ibn Abbas: He was asked why the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله nicknamed Ali (عليّاً) as Abu Turab (أبا تراب). He said: "Because he is the master of the earth (صاحب الأرض) and Allah's Proof (حجّة الله) over its inhabitants after him. Its permanence belongs to him, and its tranquility (سكونها) is due to him." He said: "And I certainly heard the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله say: 'When the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) comes, and the disbeliever (الكافر) sees what Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, has prepared for the followers (شيعة) of Ali (عليّ) in terms of reward (الثواب), proximity (الزُّلفى), and honor (الكرامة), he will say: يا لَيْتَنِي كُنْتُ تُراباً (I wish I were dust!), meaning: I wish I were one of the followers (شيعة) of Ali (عليّ) عليه السلام.'" And that is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَيَقُولُ الْكافِرُ يا لَيْتَنِي كُنْتُ تُراباً (and the faithless one will say, 'I wish I were dust!'). And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) narrated something close to its meaning. In Thawab al-A'mal (ثواب الأعمال) and al-Majma' (المجمع), narrated from Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Whoever recites ‘Amma yatasā’alūn (عمّ يتساءلون) will not complete his year—if he is constant in reciting it every day—without visiting the Sacred House of Allah (بيت الله الحرام), if Allah wills."
